description: Curated, publicly available PDF corpus to seed step 1 (identification of technology drivers) of the technology-foresight pipeline. All entries are from regulators, standards bodies, intergovernmental organisations, government agencies, industry associations, or open-access scientific repositories (arXiv). No paywalled or restricted material.
license_notice: Each PDF is the intellectual property of its respective publisher. Verify the licence/terms on the source page before redistribution. This manifest stores only links and metadata.

categories.B_regional_regulator: Regional regulators and administrations (Ofcom, BNetzA, CEPT/ECC)
categories.F_academic_ai_ml: Academic surveys on AI/ML for spectrum monitoring and sharing (arXiv, open access)
categories.G_academic_sensing: Academic work on distributed / crowdsourced / UAV-based sensing (arXiv, open access)

sources.title: Handbook on Spectrum Monitoring (Edition 5)
sources.why_relevant: The canonical international reference on regulatory spectrum monitoring. Defines the discipline, methods, equipment classes, and the relationship to spectrum management. Mandatory baseline for any driver identification.

sources.title: Final Acts WRC-23 (World Radiocommunication Conference 2023)
sources.why_relevant: Sets the international regulatory frame for the next ~4 years (until WRC-27), including new IMT identifications, HAPS, ESIMs, GMDSS modernisation. Defines what regulators will be asked to monitor.

sources.title: Report ITU-R SM.2542-0 - Next generation spectrum monitoring: proactive, autonomous and data-driven
sources.why_relevant: The most explicit ITU-published forward-looking document on where spectrum monitoring is going. Names proactive/autonomous/data-driven monitoring and AI/ML as the next-generation paradigm - effectively a checklist of driver candidates.

sources.title: Spectrum Roadmap: Delivering Ofcom's Spectrum Management Strategy
sources.manual_reason: Ofcom is behind a Cloudflare bot-challenge that returns HTTP 403 to scripted clients. Open the URL in a normal browser and save the PDF as the target filename into pdfs/.
sources.why_relevant: A national regulator's explicit forward-looking roadmap. Lists priority projects, market/technology trends, sandboxes, and 'better data for spectrum management' as a theme - a direct driver candidate.

sources.title: Space Spectrum Strategy
sources.manual_reason: Ofcom is behind a Cloudflare bot-challenge that returns HTTP 403 to scripted clients. Open the URL in a normal browser and save the PDF as the target filename into pdfs/.
sources.why_relevant: Covers the satellite / NGSO dimension of monitoring and the challenges of regulating space-based emitters.

sources.title: ECC Report 15 - Market Surveillance, Radio Equipment Inspection, Interference Investigation, Spectrum Monitoring and the Enforcement Aspects of These Activities
sources.manual_reason: docdb.cept.org presents a TLS certificate chain that Python's CA store (and certifi) does not validate. A normal browser accepts it. Open the URL in a browser and save the PDF as the target filename into pdfs/.
sources.why_relevant: European handbook tying monitoring to enforcement; explicitly maps the operational ecosystem (market surveillance, interference investigation, monitoring).

sources.title: ECC Recommendation (05)01 - Harmonisation of Automatic Measuring Methods and Data Transfer for Frequency Band Registrations
sources.manual_reason: docdb.cept.org TLS chain not validated by Python/certifi. Open in a browser and save as the target filename into pdfs/.
sources.why_relevant: Standardises automated measurement methods and data formats across European administrations - directly relevant to the 'distributed sensor networks' and 'data interoperability' driver families.

sources.title: ECC Report 160 - Enforcement Benchmarking in the Year 2010
sources.manual_reason: docdb.cept.org TLS chain not validated by Python/certifi. Open in a browser and save as the target filename into pdfs/.
sources.why_relevant: Statistical benchmark of national monitoring organisations (staff, equipment, coordination). Useful baseline for sizing-related drivers (resource intensity, automation pressure).

sources.title: Frequenz-Kompass (Frequency Compass), English edition
sources.why_relevant: German regulator's English-language statement of spectrum-distribution priorities. Captures national-regulator perspective on demand drivers.

sources.title: National Spectrum Strategy - Implementation Plan
sources.why_relevant: The US national plan, including an explicit pillar on spectrum-monitoring R&D and AI/ML, distributed sensing, common metadata standards.

sources.title: National Spectrum Strategy 37 GHz Spectrum Sharing Report
sources.why_relevant: Concrete worked example of co-equal federal/non-federal sharing in mmWave - shows the kind of monitoring infrastructure that future regulators will need.

sources.title: Enforcement Bureau Report on the PIRATE Act (Preventing Illegal Radio Abuse Through Enforcement Act)
sources.why_relevant: Concrete view of contemporary enforcement against unauthorised emitters - illustrates the legal-political driver dimension of monitoring.

sources.title: Enforcement Bureau Overview
sources.why_relevant: Structural description of how a major regulator organises spectrum enforcement and monitoring divisions.

sources.title: RSPG Opinion on the UHF band (470-694 MHz) strategy beyond 2030 (RSPG23-035)
sources.publisher: Radio Spectrum Policy Group, European Commission
sources.why_relevant: Forward-looking 10-year European view on a specific contested band - useful concrete driver candidate (PMSE vs. broadcasting vs. mobile pressure).

sources.title: RSPG Report on 6G strategic vision (RSPG25-006)
sources.publisher: Radio Spectrum Policy Group, European Commission
sources.why_relevant: Most recent EU-level forward-looking document on 6G; touches sensing/communication convergence (ISAC), NTN, sharing - prime driver-identification material.

sources.title: RSPG Opinion on how to master Europe's digital infrastructure needs (RSPG24-019)
sources.publisher: Radio Spectrum Policy Group, European Commission
sources.why_relevant: Strategic framing of spectrum within EU digital sovereignty - captures political/economic driver dimension.

sources.title: 5G Spectrum Positions
sources.publisher: GSMA (Global System for Mobile Communications Association)
sources.why_relevant: Mobile-industry consensus view on spectrum demand to 2030 - represents one of the strongest commercial pressures the regulator must monitor.

sources.title: The Road to 5G Networks - Experience to date and future developments
sources.why_relevant: OECD policy framing of spectrum-allocation evolution; useful for the policy/economic driver axis.

sources.title: Communication Regulators of the Future
sources.why_relevant: Directly relevant: how regulators are expected to evolve their tools and mandates - including monitoring - in the next decade.

sources.title: RFML Ecosystem: Considerations for the Application of Deep Learning to Spectrum Situational Awareness
sources.why_relevant: Foundational survey on Radio-Frequency Machine Learning as applied to spectrum awareness; explicitly discusses data, trust, hardware/software issues - rich driver source.

sources.title: Review of AI/ML-based Automatic Modulation Recognition (AMR)
sources.why_relevant: AMR is the workhorse task of automated monitoring; this is a recent benchmark/review covering state of the art.

sources.title: A Comprehensive Survey on Radio-Frequency Fingerprinting: Traditional Approaches, Deep Learning, and Open Challenges
sources.why_relevant: Specific-emitter identification / RF fingerprinting is a major emerging capability with both monitoring and security implications.

sources.title: Systematic Literature Review of AI-enabled Spectrum Management in 6G and Future Networks
sources.why_relevant: Recent systematic review of 110 primary studies on AI-enabled spectrum management - direct source of named technology drivers and metrics.

sources.title: Machine Learning for Spectrum Sharing: A Survey
sources.why_relevant: Covers sensing, allocation, access, handoff for 5G/6G - all of which create new monitoring obligations.

sources.title: Deep Learning for Cognitive Radio Networks toward B5G/6G
sources.why_relevant: Bridges cognitive-radio / DSA research with operational monitoring needs in next-generation networks.

sources.title: Electrosense: Open and Big Spectrum Data
sources.why_relevant: Seminal paper on collaborative, low-cost, crowdsourced spectrum monitoring - the prototype of a 'distributed regulator-grade sensing network'.

sources.title: Crowdsourced Wireless Spectrum Anomaly Detection
sources.why_relevant: Concrete ML pipeline for detecting unexpected behaviour across distributed sensors - illustrates the crowdsourced + automated anomaly-detection driver.

sources.title: Three-Dimensional Spectrum Occupancy Measurement using UAV
sources.why_relevant: UAV-based 3D spectrum measurement campaign - representative of the aerial-platforms driver for next-generation monitoring.

sources.title: Spectrum Surveying: Active Radio Map Estimation with Autonomous UAVs
sources.why_relevant: Combines model-based Bayesian estimation with deep learning and UAV path planning to build radio environment maps - directly relevant to autonomous monitoring drivers.

sources.title: ENISA Threat Landscape for 5G Networks (Update)
sources.why_relevant: Defines the security threats against 5G radio infrastructure - drivers around RF security, jamming, spoofing, integrity monitoring.

sources.title: Security in 5G Specifications - Controls in 3GPP Security Specifications (5G SA)
sources.why_relevant: Standards-side counterpart to the threat landscape - what controls exist, where the gaps are.

sources.title: NASCTN CBRS SEA Task 2 Draft Test Plan (Sharing Ecosystem Assessment)
sources.why_relevant: Concrete measurement-and-monitoring framework for a real shared band (CBRS), including noise-floor monitoring and dynamic protection areas - exemplar of operational sharing-monitoring.
